guest: Three entrepreneurs: Alex (Haven Lifestyles), Brian (Autopilot), Josh (outdoor business)

business_ideas.idea: Real estate listing lookbooks mailed by income/property value to 40 US/Canada zones.
Autopilot service replicating hedge fund and institutional investor portfolios in real-time.
Outdoor/stunt business with viral growth potential through VC funding.

predictions.text: We will hit $100 million revenue by March next year.
Small 5-million dollar businesses are equally awesome as billion-dollar companies.

dynamics: Sam and Shaan showcase three outsider businesses to demonstrate abundance of opportunity. Each founder leads with revenue number, then hosts probe on growth, adjacent opportunities, and how to help. Conversational, discovery-focused; Shaan reflects on mindset shift from scarcity to abundance.
frameworks: Start with a number, then explain business model to avoid vagueness
Adjacent opportunity analysis - see niches from market position
Root for everybody mindset - you never lose when supporting others
Lifestyle business vs VC-scale startup trade-offs
learnings: Niches most people ignore can generate $10-30M revenue without hype
Real estate agent magazines work because agents need proof of advertising reach
0 to 1 million is harder than 1 to 30 million in revenue
Abundance mindset unlocks pattern-matching across unrelated domains
High-touch sales (door-to-door) still works for boring B2B products

banger_quotes.quote: I started as a bill collector calling for money, great sales training.
Getting from 0 to 1 million was the hardest thing I ever did.
Opportunity is abundant. It's about picking what fits you.

numbers_dropped: Haven Lifestyles: $10 million revenue, $2.5M profit, 10 years
Autopilot: $30 million revenue, 70M run rate, heading to $100M
40 publications covering all US and Canada
notable: Shaan emphasizes learning from everyone and stealing good traits without copying whole lifestyle.
